nodes.label: AI as source of creative and insightful examples
nodes.label_negative: AI generating basic or unchallenging examples
nodes.grounding_span: sometimes AI comes up with creative and illuminating examples

nodes.label: AI reliability and accuracy in generating content
nodes.label_negative: AI generating nonsense or false claims
nodes.grounding_span: when the model generates nonsense questions like asking to prove a false claim

nodes.label: AI as collaborative partner requiring human oversight
nodes.label_negative: AI as autonomous replacement for human work
nodes.grounding_span: Always collaboration, I would never hand something off entirely to AI

nodes.label: AI use in maintaining authentic human connection in teaching
nodes.label_negative: AI use that undermines teacher-student relationship
nodes.grounding_span: it does mean something to read the words of a real human when developing a bond of trust

nodes.label: AI as occasional resource for specific tasks
nodes.label_negative: AI as integral part of daily workflow
nodes.grounding_span: I don't often use AI through the course of my workday, but rather on an as-needed basis

nodes.label: Intellectual engagement and deeper understanding
nodes.grounding_span: if the problem is something like a 'plug-and-chug' ... that's not particularly helpful in driving a deeper understanding

nodes.label: Authentic human connection and trust in teaching
nodes.grounding_span: developing a bond of trust that is crucial to teacher-student relationship

nodes.label: Professional autonomy and personal engagement
nodes.grounding_span: I prefer to do on my own so that I'm more engaged with the students

nodes.label: Satisfaction when AI generates insightful examples
nodes.grounding_span: when I'm able to have a LLM generate an interesting and insightful example problem -- that's really satisfying

nodes.label: Frustration with AI generating nonsense or false claims
nodes.grounding_span: when the model generates nonsense questions like asking to prove a false claim, that can be quite frustrating

nodes.label: Skepticism toward AI replacing teaching roles
nodes.grounding_span: it seems silly to suggest AI could take over much, if any, aspect of teaching in a meaningful way

nodes.label: Openness to expanding AI use if reliability and environmental concerns improve
nodes.grounding_span: If it can do more things and be more reliable and less harmful to the environment then sure, why wouldn't I find ways to expand my usage

nodes.label: Quality assessment through personal problem-solving experience
nodes.grounding_span: base the assessment of quality of example on how challenging it was for me or what neat tricks or ideas it might require

nodes.label: Collaborative refinement through iterative prompting
nodes.grounding_span: I just ask for example problems testing for X, Y, and Z. Then I may follow up with clarifying prompts
